text:
$MSFT says Xbox console storage and memory prices have “increased by more than 2.5x” and expects “another doubling by the fall of 2027.”

Very bullish for $MU, Samsung and SK Hynix as the same scarce memory supply is now commanding premium pricing across AI, mobile, PC and console markets at the same time.

$MSFT raising Xbox console prices worldwide citing storage and memory costs: 

• Xbox Series S 512GB from $399.99 to $499.99
• Xbox Series S 1TB from $449.99 to $599.99
• Xbox Series X 1TB Digital from $599.99 to $749.99
• Xbox Series X 1TB Disc Drive from $649.99 to $799.99

$GOOGL upgraded Google Finance with AI-powered portfolio tracking that can build portfolios from screenshots, CSVs, PDFs or plain-language descriptions.

It also added scheduled market briefings, AI stock-move explanations and a new Android app with iOS coming later this year.
$MU generated ~$365M in profit per day last quarter  🤯

Right now the space names are in the hangover phase from the $SPCX halo effect where investors who chased the move are taking profits and rotating elsewhere.

This has caused a major pullback across the whole bucket in the past month: 
• $RKLB -46%
• $PL -50%
• $ASTS -52% 
• $BKSY -53%
• $RDW -59% 
• $FLY -59%
• $LUNR -60%

I think this is just the space trade moving from excitement-driven multiple expansion into a more selective phase.

The hype trade is cooling off but the long-term theme is still intact and next leg will be led by the companies that can prove real execution beyond the SpaceX halo.

You can watch my full $MU earnings breakdown here:

piped.video/Pqp-Sz08LFc?si=pVRp…

$MU customers are basically telling Micron that memory is no longer something they can casually buy later because AI has turned it into a strategic resource that needs to be secured years in advance.

When customers put down deposits, sign multi-year agreements and accept minimum pricing then they're taking on part of the cyclical risk that Micron used to carry alone.

Thats why I think Micron deserves to trade at least 15x earnings because a memory company with no visibility deserves a commodity multiple but a memory company with customer deposits, take-or-pay commitments, minimum pricing and multi-year supply agreements deserves more credit for earnings durability.
$IREN will have its logo on Golden State Warriors jerseys next season.
$BMNR is now in a 90% drawdown over the past year  😳
$AAPL is down nearly 5% after raising prices on select iPads and Macs due to memory chip costs with Apple saying it has “never seen” component prices rise this much this quickly.
$MU is now worth more than both $META and $TSLA.

$GOOGL is reorganizing its AI coding team as it tries to catch Anthropic in one of AI’s most lucrative markets.

The move comes after departures of Noam Shazeer to OpenAI and John Jumper to Anthropic adding pressure around AI talent, coding performance and compute allocation.

$AAPL says AI data centers have created an “extraordinary surge” in memory and storage demand driving component price increases it has “never seen” move this much this quickly.

Apple said it has absorbed the pressure so far but now needs to begin raising prices.

MACRO DATA JUST DROPPED

• PCE 4.1% YoY vs. Est. 4.1%
• Core PCE 3.4% YoY vs. Est 3.4%

• Personal spending +0.7% MoM vs. Est. +0.6%
• Personal income +0.7% MoM vs. Est. +0.4%

• U.S. Q1 Final GDP +2.1 vs. Est. +1.6%

$MU DRAM revenue is up 10x in less than three years showing how AI has turned memory into the bottleneck of all bottlenecks in this infrastructure buildout.

The next leg is agentic AI where autonomous systems need to access, process and learn from massive amounts of data which makes standard and low-power DRAM increasingly critical around CPUs.

$NVDA CPU-only Vera racks show where this is headed with each rack expected to use 400TB of DRAM alone (a 5-10x increase versus other Nvidia GPU-based racks).

Hormuz traffic sees a sharp d/d uptick

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings rose to 70 on 24 June, up 105% day on day, as demining efforts advanced and operators increasingly used the Omani route. Commercial traffic accounted for most activity, with 53 transits, while low-risk vessels dominated the day’s profile. The US-Iran MoU framework and apparent lifting of the US blockade appear to have supported a short-term confidence boost, although IRGC warnings against use of the Omani route could create a new source of contention.

IMO-route use picked up slightly, but continued Dark routing, incomplete demining and unresolved issues over inspections, sanctions and future Strait governance mean the rebound is not yet a confirmed return to pre-crisis conditions.

Micron $MU guided for FQ4 revenue to be $50 billion at midpoint, up 342% YoY and nearly 21% QoQ, coming in well above estimates for $43.45 billion.

Adjusted EPS was guided to be $31, up 923% YoY.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

Micron $MU reported FQ3 revenue up 345.8% YoY and 73.7% QoQ to $41.46 billion, beating estimates by $6.21 billion.

Operating margin surpassed 80%, while adjusted EPS rose 1,215% YoY to $25.11, well ahead of the $20.28 estimate.

$NVDA $AMD $AVGO

In 2027, the WSTS projects global semiconductor sales to reach $1.91 trillion, with memory revenue surpassing the $1 trillion mark and growing 32% YoY.
